history: The client has a master's degree in engineering and experience in robotics. They have expressed past remorse about not dedicating enough time to a project, suggesting a tendency toward self-criticism and perfectionism. This pattern of self-blame and regret may contribute to heightened sensitivity to perceived rejection or failure in new situations.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
intermediate_belief: If someone doesn't respond to me, it means I did something wrong or I am not worthy of their attention. I must always perform perfectly or others will lose interest in me. If I make a mistake or am not good enough, I will be rejected.
intermediate_belief_depression: If people don't respond to me, it proves that I am not worth caring about and nothing I do will change that.
coping_strategies: The client repeatedly checks their email for a response and may seek reassurance from friends or family. They may also withdraw socially or avoid further contact to protect themselves from possible rejection. Sometimes, they distract themselves with work or other activities to avoid thinking about the situation.
situation: Sent an email to someone they are interested in dating and received no response.
auto_thought: They must not be interested in me. I probably said something wrong and now they don't want to talk to me.
behavior: I check my email repeatedly to see if they've replied. I avoid sending another message because I'm afraid of being rejected. I might talk to a friend about what happened to get reassurance.
